methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $400M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 53.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
ClearSpace, a Series A space and aerospace company founded by Luc Piguet, is valued at $400M post-money following $36M in total capital raised. The valuation is assessed against a comp universe of 5 public space and aerospace companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Planet Labs.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to Series B advancement, yielding $800M. Peer-median triangulation across 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies—Aalyria ($1.3B Series B), Hubble Network ($400M Series B), and Sift ($274M Series B)—produces a $400M median with a $274M-$1.3B range. Public-comp anchoring using the sector's 53.2x median price-to-sales cannot be applied absent the target's disclosed revenue.

The two-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $340M to $920M, with a median of $600M. This suggests the current $400M valuation sits in the lower quartile relative to peer comparables and stage-typical progression. Key uncertainties include ClearSpace's unit economics and revenue trajectory (unavailable), which would materially refine the public-comp multiple approach. The wide peer valuation dispersion ($274M-$1.3B) also reflects meaningful differences in market positioning within the space sector.
